Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Known biosynthetic genes catalyzing O-methylation to produce HDMBOA-Glc; GWAS confirmed their association on chromosome 1 with HDMBOA-Glc abundance; this locus also constitutes a metabolite QTL hotspot regulating multiple benzoxazinoid mass features
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: GWAS mapping confirmed the Bx10-12 locus on chromosome 1 as the most significantly associated region for HDMBOA-Glc; the locus corresponds to a metabolite QTL hotspot in both leaf tips and bases (Figure 9B, Figure 8); biallelic haplotype analysis showed additive effects with a second locus on chromosome 9 (Figure 9C)
Genes.Genetic_Variation: Haplotypic variation between tropical and temperate maize lines; tropical lines accumulate more HDMBOA-Glc while temperate lines accumulate more DIMBOA-Glc
Genes.Natural_Variation_Impact: Different alleles at Bx10-12 contribute to differential HDMBOA-Glc vs DIMBOA-Glc accumulation across maize subpopulations
Genes.Notes: Paralogous gene cluster spanning ~110 kb on chromosome 1; previously characterized by Meihls et al. (2013) and Handrick et al. (2016)
Genes.Tissue_Specificity: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases

Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Known biosynthetic gene for DIM₂BOA-Glc; GWAS confirmed its association on chromosome 2
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: GWAS mapping confirmed the Bx13 locus on chromosome 2 as significantly associated with DIM₂BOA-Glc abundance, with most significantly associated SNPs in linkage disequilibrium with the gene (Figure 9A)
Genes.Genetic_Variation: Natural variation at this locus affects DIM₂BOA-Glc accumulation
Genes.Notes: Previously characterized by Handrick et al. (2016)

Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Previously unknown locus on chromosome 9 affecting HDMBOA-Glc accumulation; acts additively with the Bx10-12 locus on chromosome 1
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: GWAS identified a novel locus on chromosome 9 with most significantly associated SNPs in a single 25-kb LD block containing the 3' region of GRMZM2G108309; haplotype analysis showed additive effect with Bx10-12 on HDMBOA-Glc content (Figure 9C); expression levels differed significantly between haplotypes (Figure 9D); the 20 inbred lines with highest vs lowest expression showed significant difference in HDMBOA-Glc content (Figure 9E)
Genes.Genetic_Variation: Biallelic haplotypes at the 25-kb LD block on chromosome 9; expression levels significantly differ between alleles
Genes.Natural_Variation_Impact: Additive effect with Bx10-12 locus; contributes to natural variation in HDMBOA-Glc content across the diversity panel
Genes.Notes: Newly identified in this study; all associated SNPs are downstream of the gene; correlation between expression and HDMBOA-Glc was significant only when comparing extreme expression groups, not across the entire population

Genes.Downstream_Target: Phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid ester isomers (coumaroyl, caffeoyl, feruloyl conjugates with 2-hydroxycitric acid)
Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Coding-sequence variation in this gene determines which structural isomers (more polar 2-O-acylated vs less polar putative 3-O-acylated) of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters accumulate; tropical maize lines produce less polar isomers while temperate lines produce more polar isomers
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: GWAS mapped all three pairs of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid ester isomers to a single 10-kb LD block on chromosome 4 contained within GRMZM2G063909 (Figure 11C-E); expression levels did not differ between isomer-accumulating groups, suggesting structural (coding) variation is causal (Figure 11F); cosegregation was independently validated using B73×Ki11 and B73×CML247 near-isogenic lines showing perfect cosegregation between genotype at GRMZM2G063909 and isomer type (Figure 12, Supplemental Figure 7); heterozygotes showed intermediate phenotypes producing both isomers
Genes.Genetic_Variation: Coding sequence variation; all linked SNPs are in the coding region; tropical vs temperate alleles produce different structural isomers
Genes.Natural_Variation_Impact: Tropical maize lines accumulate less polar (putative 3-O-acylated) isomers while temperate lines accumulate more polar (2-O-acylated) isomers; the two isomer groups are generally mutually exclusive
Genes.Notes: Annotated as ortholog of Arabidopsis and rice citrate synthase genes; corresponds to the metabolite QTL hotspot on chromosome 4; NMR confirmed the more polar isomers as 2-O-acylated hydroxycitric acid esters; the less polar isomers degraded during NMR analysis
Genes.Target_Metabolite: Coumaroyl hydroxycitric acid, caffeoyl hydroxycitric acid, and feruloyl hydroxycitric acid (isomeric forms)
Genes.Tissue_Specificity: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases

Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Catalyze the first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis; significantly higher expression in leaf tips than leaf bases, consistent with flavonoid accumulation only in leaf tips
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: Transcriptomic data from Kremling et al. (2018) showed the two most strongly expressed chalcone synthase genes (GRMZM2G422750 and GRMZM2G380650) have significantly higher expression in leaf tips vs bases (FDR < 0.05; Figure 3B); UV absorbance chromatograms confirmed complete absence of flavonoid peaks in leaf base samples (Figure 3A)
Genes.Genetic_Variation: Five chalcone synthase genes in the B73 reference genome; two are most strongly expressed
Genes.Natural_Variation_Impact: Tissue-specific expression contributes to the absence of flavonoids in leaf bases and their presence in leaf tips
Genes.Notes: Used to explain the tissue-type metabolomic differentiation driven by flavonoid abundance; expression data from published transcriptomic resource
Genes.Tissue_Specificity: Preferentially expressed in seedling leaf tips; low expression in leaf bases

Title: Metabolome-Scale Genome-Wide Association Studies Reveal Chemical Diversity and Genetic Control of Maize Specialized Metabolites
